channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: LIVE: Real Madrid 1-1 Girona – La Liga
item.description: All the updates, with post-match reaction and interviews, following our text commentary of the game at Estadio Bernabeu.

item.title: Muslims hold first Friday prayers at Al-Aqsa Mosque after reopening
item.description: Thousands of Muslims performed Friday prayers at Al-Aqsa Mosque after it reopened following a 40-day closure by Israel.

item.title: Real Madrid hand Barcelona huge La Liga title chance after draw with Girona
item.description: Real Madrid draw means Barcelona can move nine points clear at the top of La Liga if they beat Espanyol on Saturday.

item.title: Magnolia CEO: Aiming for Healthy Traffic in US Expansion
item.description: Magnolia Bakery CEO Nathan Louer discusses US franchise expansion, remaining an ‘attainable luxury,’ and why driving traffic is the key to the company’s long term strategy. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Bailey Lipschultz on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Blackstone Files for IPO of Data Center Acquisition Firm
item.description: Blackstone Inc. filed for an initial public offering of a new data-center acquisition vehicle that plans to buy already-built and leased properties benefiting from the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Wall Street Seizes on Private Credit Fears With Way to Short
item.description: Wall Street is debuting a new product that will give investors a way to wager against private credit.

item.title: Hormuz, Lebanon Central to US, Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Bloomberg's Jeff Mason said that the bar for success in this weekend's peace talks between the US and Iran, is that both parties stay at the table and they don't 'fail,' potentially ending the tenuous two-week ceasefire. Mason said that Israel's strikes on Lebanon and oil flow through the Strait of Hormuz are the key hurdles as the two countries try to negotiate a longer term deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: It’s Not Just Oil. A Blocked Hormuz Risks Hiking the Price of Nearly Everything You Buy
item.description: As war in Iran crimps global oil supplies, it is hitting a range of other commodities and promising to remake global supply chains.

item.title: Worker who allegedly set fire to California warehouse compares self to Luigi Mangione
item.description:
Chamel Abdulkarim was charged with felony and state arson counts after allegedly destroying property worth nearly $600m

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

An employee who allegedly burned down a California warehouse compared himself to Luigi Mangione in a message to co-workers after setting the fire, according to authorities in a Friday press conference, the Los Angeles Times reported.

Chamel Abdulkarim, 29, has been charged with several counts of felony and state arson after a massive fire destroyed the Kimberly-Clark paper products warehouse in Ontario, California, about an hour outside of Los Angeles.

Continue reading...

item.title: Bard College president tells staff he will soon be cleared in inquiry over Epstein ties
item.description:
WilmerHale, the law firm examining Leon Botstein’s communications with Jeffrey Epstein, said review is ‘ongoing’

Bard College’s president, Leon Botstein, told his staff at a meeting this week that there was no way he could have known that Jeffrey Epstein – who was a convicted sex offender during their interactions over the years – was actually “reprehensible” and predicted he would soon be cleared of any hint of impropriety about their relationship, according to sources who witnessed the remarks.

The liberal arts college’s board of trustees hired in February the white-shoe law firm WilmerHale to independently investigate communications between Epstein and Botstein.

Continue reading...
